qa_pairs.question: How can I disable the NVIDIA Data Center GPU Manager (DCGM) to enable GPU profiling on Narval or Rorqual?
qa_pairs.answer: To disable the NVIDIA Data Center GPU Manager (DCGM) for GPU profiling on Narval or Rorqual, you must set the `DISABLE_DCGM` environment variable to `1` during job submission. An example command would be: `DISABLE_DCGM=1 salloc --account=def-someuser --gpus-per-node=a100:1 --mem=4000M --time=03:00`.

qa_pairs.question: After submitting a job with DCGM disabled for profiling, how do I confirm that DCGM is no longer running on the node?
qa_pairs.answer: After submitting your interactive job with DCGM disabled, you should wait until DCGM is no longer active on the node by using the command: `while [ ! -z "$(dcgmi -v | grep 'Hostengine build info:')" ]; do  sleep 5; done`. Once this command completes, you can proceed to launch your profiler.

qa_pairs.question: On which clusters is GPU profiling, requiring the disabling of DCGM, currently available?
qa_pairs.answer: GPU profiling that requires disabling DCGM is currently possible only on Narval and Rorqual. It is not yet available on Fir and Nibi.
